methods.description: This method establishes an age model for Core 17286-1 using radiocarbon dating and Bayesian age modeling. It involves determining radiocarbon ages of foraminifera samples, calibrating these ages, and correlating them with a global benthic reference record to create a comprehensive chronological framework.

steps.description: The radiocarbon (14C) ages of 13 samples of mixed planktonic foraminifera were determined by accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) 14C dating at the Leibniz Laboratory for Radiometric Dating and Stable Isotope Research at Kiel University.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain radiocarbon ages using AMS 14C dating

steps.description: The obtained conventional AMS 14C ages were calibrated using OxCal 4.3 with the Marine13 calibration curve and a marine reservoir correction for the northern BoB.

To further constrain the core chronology, we used the approach conventionally applied to Quaternary deep-sea sediments, correlating with a chronologically well-constrained δ18O benthic reference record.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate with δ18O benthic reference record

steps.description: The final age model of Core 17286-1 was established by Bayesian age modeling using a P_Sequence depositional model with variable parameter k and outlier analysis implemented in OxCal 4.3.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Bayesian age modeling using OxCal 4.3

steps.description: To consider the inherent chronological uncertainty of the LS16 global δ18O benthic stack, we incorporated the published 2σ age uncertainties of the 11 tie points in the model.
